questions.question: What is |−8|?
questions.explanation: |−8| = 8 because absolute value is the distance from zero. −8 is 8 units away from zero, so |−8| = 8.

questions.question: Which pair has the same absolute value?
questions.explanation: |-3| = 3 and |3| = 3. Opposite numbers always have the same absolute value because they are equidistant from zero.

questions.question: Solve: |x| = 0.
questions.explanation: |x| = 0 means the distance from x to zero is zero. Only x = 0 satisfies this.

questions.question: Why does |x| = −5 have no solution?
options: Negative numbers are not allowed in equations
Absolute value is always non-negative, so it cannot equal a negative number
questions.explanation: Absolute value represents distance, which is always ≥ 0. A negative number can never be a distance.
